Title: Dunlop revs up Malaysia expansion plans
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dunlop is expanding its presence in Malaysia by growing its dealer network, widening its tyre portfolio and strengthening partnerships with automotive service providers.
The global tyre brand, managed by Toyotsu Binter Malaysia Sdn Bhd and backed by Japan's Sumitomo Rubber Industries, sees Malaysia as a key growth market despite production being shifted overseas.
Toyotsu Binter Malaysia managing director Marcus Lim said Dunlop has expanded its nationwide dealer network to about 250 outlets from around 150 since May.
The expansion reflects stronger support from dealers and automotive service partners as the company rebuilds its presence in the Malaysian market.
"Currently we have about 250 dealers in Malaysia. I think the maximum number is a bit hard for me to ascertain right now.
"Of course, we do hope to have more. But we need to have more that makes sense, because having too many also cannot be optimal," he said at a press conference today.
Dunlop, which established Malaysia's first local tyre manufacturing facility in 1963 is now focusing on strengthening its position in the competitive replacement tyre market.
Lim said the company sees opportunities amid rising demand for tyres offering safety, performance and value.
"Malaysia is an important market for Dunlop and we see tremendous opportunities for long-term growth.
"Motorists are looking for products that deliver quality, safety and performance, but they also expect genuine value. That is where we see Dunlop's opportunity," he said.
Beyond the replacement market, Dunlop continues supplying original equipment manufacturer (OEM) tyres for selected models from brands including Toyota, Lexus, Isuzu, Mazda and Leapmotor.
Lim said OEM supply depends on vehicle manufacturers' global production strategies, with tyre brands varying according to models, markets and production locations.
"There is no 100 per cent control of any tyre brand with any manufacturer. That is the reality of how supply chain works for these OEM manufacturers.
"The replacement business is an immediate business for us. We are able to address consumers' product needs immediately and introduce different products to different customer segments.
"The OEM business takes more time because it depends on vehicle development cycles and manufacturer approvals," Lim said.
Dunlop tyres are currently available at all UMW Toyota dealerships nationwide as the company expands its replacement tyre distribution channels.
The company has also expanded its product range to cover various vehicle segments, including passenger cars, sport utility vehicles (SUVs) and electric vehicles (EVs).
Its current lineup includes the Blue Response TG for passenger vehicles, SP Sport Maxx 060+ for performance driving and Grandtrek R/T01 for SUVs and pickup trucks.
Dunlop is also preparing to introduce the e.Sport Maxx tyre, developed specifically to meet the requirements of EV users.
Sumitomo Rubber Industries Asean managing director Takehiko Asai said the company continues investing in next-generation tyre technologies.
These include improvements in energy efficiency, wet-weather grip, rolling resistance and cabin noise to meet evolving consumer needs.
Asai said Malaysia remains a high-potential market despite the dominance of locally manufactured vehicles, supported by demand from foreign brands, imported vehicles and emerging EV models.
Meanwhile, Dunlop is exploring the establishment of dedicated retail outlets with selected dealer partners through its proposed Dunlop Performance Centres.
The company said the initiative would strengthen its retail presence while offering customers a more focused brand experience.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police probe viral bus stop stabbing near Wangsa Maju university
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating a viral video showing a man being attacked with a sharp weapon while waiting at a bus stop near a university in Wangsa Maju.
Wangsa Maju district police chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman confirmed that police received a report from the victim.
"An investigation paper has been opened under Section 324 of the Penal Code and no arrests have been made so far," he said when contacted.
The viral video shows a man dressed in black holding a sharp weapon suddenly attacking a man in white, repeatedly kicking and stabbing him.
The victim is believed to have sustained injuries during the incident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PSSC sets two-week deadline for govt stance on Lynas US defence ties
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has been given a two-week deadline to issue its official position on Lynas Rare Earths' supply agreements with the United States defence industry, amid concerns over the deal's implications for Malaysia's interests and foreign policy stance.
The Parliamentary Special Select Committee (PSSC) on International Relations and International Trade said Putrajaya must provide clarity on its approach to reassure the public of its direction in addressing the issue.
In a statement today, the committee recommended that the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry, Foreign Ministry, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry, and Science, Technology and Innovation Ministry seek legal advice from the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) on the agreements to safeguard Malaysia's interests.
Chaired by Subang member of Parliament Wong Chen, the committee said the recommendations followed a meeting to examine Lynas' rare earth operations and supply agreements with the Pentagon, including their potential implications for Malaysia.
Wong said the meeting held on July 16 was called to assess concerns that the agreement could affect Malaysia's reputation as a supporter of Palestine.
The committee also reviewed Malaysia's foreign direct investment (FDI) policies and discussed the need for a national policy framework for the rare earth industry, which it described as having significant economic potential.
The meeting also heard presentations from various organisations, including the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations (Mapim), Sahabat Alam Malaysia, BDS Malaysia and Greenpeace Malaysia, before Lynas representatives briefed members and responded to questions on the company's operations and its role in the US defence supply chain.
This was followed by a session between members of Parliament and representatives of the ministries to discuss Malaysia's position comprehensively, taking into account the country's domestic and international interests.
The committee will publish an official report detailing the discussions and recommendations, which Wong said would demonstrate Parliament's check-and-balance function.
Earlier on July 7,
Lynas said it would invest about US$35 million
in a permanent magnet facility near its Kuantan site in partnership with South Korea's JS Link.
The plant is expected to produce 3,000 tonnes of magnets annually using neodymium, iron and boron, linking Lynas' rare earth production with downstream manufacturing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah residents told not to panic over low-flying military aircraft, says RMAF
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The public in parts of Sabah's east coast have been advised not to panic if they observe low-flying aircraft or the presence of defence and security assets as the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) conducts an exercise beginning Monday.
The RMAF said Exercise Kelawar, which will be conducted until July 29, would involve tactical low-altitude flights and maritime asset movements in designated training areas covering Tawau, Semporna and Lahad Datu under the Op Pasir Selatan area.
"Residents in the affected areas are urged to remain calm and not be alarmed by the increased presence of military aircraft and security assets during the exercise.
"The exercise is aimed at enhancing the competency, efficiency and operational readiness of RMAF helicopter crews and assets through tactical flight operations in facing conflict scenarios and non-traditional threats, particularly during night operations," it said in a statement.
The exercise will also involve cooperation from special forces elements from the three services, namely the RMAF Special Forces, Royal Malaysian Navy Special Forces and the Army's Special Service Group.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Social media influencer charged with raping underage girl
Article:
KANGAR: A 23-year-old social media influencer was charged at the Sessions Court today with raping an underage girl last month.
Muhammad Iman Hazriq Azizan pleaded not guilty after the charge was read out before Judge Sharifah Norazlita Syed Salim Idid.
According to the charge sheet, he allegedly raped the victim, who was aged 14 years and 10 months at the time of the incident, in a room in Taman Kangar at 3.10am on June 17.
He was charged under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code, which carries a jail term of between 10 and 30 years and whipping upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Nabila Ahmad Poad prosecuted, while the accused was unrepresented.
The court allowed bail at RM13,000 with one surety. The accused was also ordered to report to the nearest police station monthly and prohibited from contacting the victim or prosecution witnesses until the case is concluded.
Sharifah Norazlita fixed Aug 14 for mention.
The accused was arrested on July 13 after police received a report from the victim alleging that she had been raped and photographed in an obscene manner at a rented house in Taman Kangar.
Kangar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop previously said the arrest followed a report lodged by the victim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's June inflation eases to 1.9pct
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation eased to 1.9 per cent in June from 2.0 per cent in May, the Statistics Department said.
The consumer price index rose to 137.1 in June from 134.5 a year earlier.
The  moderation was mainly due to the transport group, which rose 2.8 per cent in June compared with 3.8 per cent in May.
The personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services group, as well as education, also recorded slower increases of 3.4 per cent and 2.1 per cent, respectively.
Meanwhile, inflation accelerated in the insurance and financial services group to 5.7 per cent from 4.9 per cent a year earlier. The restaurant and accommodation services group also recorded a faster increase of 2.6 per cent.
"The information and communication group rose 2.4 per cent in June. The housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels group also increased to 1.4 per cent from 1.2 per cent in May 2026," the department said in a statement.
Seven states recorded inflation above the national average, led by Pahang at 2.6 per cent, followed by Johor and Negri Sembilan at 2.5 per cent each. Labuan posted 2.3 per cent, Kuala Lumpur 2.2 per cent, while Kedah and Putrajaya both recorded 2.0 per cent.
The department said all states recorded higher inflation in the food and beverages category, except Kelantan.
On a month-on-month basis, headline inflation was unchanged in June from May.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt won't interfere in MMC Port's management appointments, says Loke
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government does not interfere in the management decisions of port operators, including the appointment of foreign executives, as long as ownership requirements involving strategic assets are maintained.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the ministry only regulates matters related to shareholding, particularly for strategic assets such as ports, which must remain majority-owned by Malaysian interests.
"As far as the government is concerned, we regulate shareholding.
"For companies holding concessions and strategic assets such as ports, they must be majority-owned by Malaysians, with at least 51 per cent Malaysian ownership," he said at a press conference at the Transport Ministry here today.
He was responding to questions on reports that Malaysian Firm MMC Port Holdings had appointed a foreign national as its executive chairman and whether the move had received government approval.
However, Loke said appointments involving the management of companies were within the discretion of the operators and did not require government approval.
"In terms of management, usually the government does not interfere in the company's management affairs.
"There are actually ports that have foreign chief executive officers. For example, Port of Tanjung Pelepas (PTP) has had foreign CEOs before.
"So, in terms of management, the government does not intervene. Companies are free to choose who they believe is suitable to manage their operations," he said.
Loke added that any changes involving ownership or shareholding would have to be reported to the relevant authorities.
"Shareholding is different. If there are any changes in shares, it must be reported to the government, including through the relevant agencies."
Asked whether the ministry had received any notification regarding a possible change in MMC Port's shareholding involving a foreign party, Loke said the ministry had not received any information from either the company or the concession holder.
"As far as we are concerned, no. We have not received any information or notification from the company or the concession."
Earlier, it was
reported
that the former chairman and CEO of logistics giant DP World, Sultan Ahmed Bin Sulayem, will take charge of MMC Port Holdings, with the group chief executive leaving the post with immediate effect, according to a memo seen by Reuters.
The move puts direct control of Malaysia's largest port operator in the hands of the Emirati, who resigned from Dubai-based DP World in February after scrutiny over email exchanges with Jeffrey Epstein that were released by the United States Justice Department.
MMC Ports is Malaysia's largest port operating group, with seven ports situated along or near the Strait of Malacca, a narrow sea lane linking the Indian Ocean and the Pacific that is vital to global trade.
The strait is one of the world's most important shipping chokepoints. The US Energy Information Administration has said the strait is among the world's most important oil transit routes by volume.
MMC Ports had been expected to pursue what could have been Malaysia's biggest IPO in more than a decade, but Reuters reported in October that the company had delayed the planned listing.
The July 12 memo, addressed to MMC Port management and the chief executives of its operating ports, said all matters that would previously have gone to the group CEO should now be sent directly to Bin Sulayem's office.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN-BN understanding boosts Pas hopes in Negri Sembilan polls
Article:
KOTA BARU: Pas is confident of winning all five state seats it is contesting in the Negri Sembilan election, following an understanding reached between Perikatan Nasional and Barisan Nasional.
Its vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said the arrangement has strengthened the party's chances, including in the two seats it is seeking to wrest, Klawang and Ampangan.
He said Pas is confident of retaining the three constituencies it currently holds, namely Bagan Pinang, Paroi and Serting.
"We are confident of defending the three seats we already hold.
"Following the understanding reached with BN, we also believe we have a good chance of winning the other two seats, Klawang and Ampangan," Amar, who is also Kelantan state speaker, said to reporters after delivering his Friday sermon at Medan Ilmu here today.
The five seats are among 11 contested by PN in the state election.
He said the party's confidence is based on the strength of its candidates and their track records.
"The three incumbents have proven records as elected representatives.
"In Ampangan, our candidate Datuk Dr Mohamad Rafie Ab Malek is a former Negeri Sembilan Umno secretary and has his own track record, which gives us confidence in his chances.
"Overall, I see winning potential in all five seats contested by Pas.
"Every one of them has a realistic chance of victory," he said.
Last night, PN announced its 11 candidates for the Negri Sembilan election, none of whom will be competing against Barisan Nasional in the Aug 1 election.
Pas will field five candidates, while new coalition component Parti Wawasan Negara will provide four, with Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party fielding one each.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiwan tech rout drags Asian equities lower
Article:
SINGAPORE: Asian equities dropped for a second day on Friday, with Taiwan bearing the brunt of a chip-stock selloff, as record AI-driven earnings failed to meet lofty investor expectations, while the volatile South Korean equity markets were shut for a holiday.
The MSCI EM Asia equities index slumped as much as 2.3 per cent, with Taiwanese stocks, which make up nearly 33 per cent of the index, dropping nearly six per cent to a five-week low. The MSCI gauge has lost nearly five per cent in two sessions so far.
The semiconductor-heavy Taiwanese benchmark declined for a second day, with TSMC, the world's top contract chipmaker and a key Nvidia and Apple supplier, losing more than five per cent to its lowest in nearly three weeks, despite record quarterly profit.
"Taiwan is one of the cleanest liquid proxies for AI chip demand. When positioning is crowded, even a strong result can trigger profit-taking if the upside surprise is not enough," said Billy Leung, investment strategist at Global X ETFs Australia.
Investors are taking another step towards scrutinising chipmakers' ability to monetise, said Glenn Yin, director of research at ACCM, adding that despite TSMC's record quarterly profit, investors are focused on higher capital expenditure and overseas expansion costs.
"In other words, beating the expectation is not sufficient unless guidance is also dialled up to justify those investments."
The chip-heavy South Korean benchmark KOSPI ended the week nearly 9 per cent in the red on Thursday, rounding off a turbulent week of sharp swings in memory chipmakers, the first rate hike in over three years, and regulatory intervention in single-stock leveraged funds that are behind much of the upheaval.
In Singapore, Southeast Asia's financial hub, equities fell as much as 0.8 per cent and extended declines from the previous session, while the currency was steady at 1.2904 a dollar.
The country's non-oil domestic exports rose by 20.7 per cent in June from a year earlier, as electronics exports continued to surge amid AI-related demand while non-electronics declined.
In neighbouring Malaysia, stocks were up 0.2 per cent while the ringgit weakened marginally to 4.081 per US dollar, its lowest since early July.
Data showed that Malaysia's inflation rose 1.9 per cent in June from a year earlier, while its official advance estimate showed that the economy grew 5.8 per cent in the second quarter from a year earlier.
Elsewhere, Thai equities drifted lower, while those in the Philippines advanced as much as 1.4 per cent, their highest since early March. Both the Thai baht and the Taiwan dollar weakened to their lowest since late April 2025 against the US dollar.
The rupiah extended gains into a fourth day, firming to 17,935 a dollar for the first time in two weeks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia higher at midday on stronger-than-expected Q2 GDP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia turned higher at midday, buoyed by advance estimates of the second quarter 2026 gross domestic product, which expanded by 5.8 per cent, following the 5.4 per cent growth in the preceding quarter.
At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 4.62 points to 1,726.81 from Thursday's close of 1,722.19.
The FBM KLCI, which opened 2.33 points weaker at 1,719.86, moved between 1,719.56 and 1,728.20 during the morning trading session.
However, on the broader market, losers surpassed gainers 618 to 347, while 468 counters were unchanged, 1,302 untraded and 12 suspended.
Turnover stood at 1.92 billion units worth RM1.33 billion.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of Equity Research Thong Pak Leng said the FBM KLCI regained its uptrend to end above the 1,720 level after a brief correction in early trade.
"We noticed foreign funds are back on domestic stocks, but overall trading activities remained rather muted. Nonetheless, we expect the index to trend within the 1,715-1,730 range today," he said.
Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd gained 18 sen to RM11.22, Public Bank Bhd added three sen to RM5.23, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd was nine sen firmer at RM7.79, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd was flat at RM14.30, but IHH Healthcare Bhd lost 13 sen to RM8.40.
As for the active stocks, Tanco Holdings Bhd rose two sen to 34 sen, Ekovest Bhd added one sen to 21 sen, NationGate Holdings Bhd slid seven sen to RM1.10, Dagang NeXchange Bhd eased two sen to 44.5 sen and VS Industry Bhd was half a sen weaker at 23 sen.
Of the top gainers, Hong Leong Bank Bhd increased 32 sen to RM22.34, Padini Holdings Bhd added 16 sen to RM1.58, Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd rose 14 sen to RM18.82, while Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd gained 13 sen to RM4.71.
Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the losers list, falling RM1 to RM44.28, while Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd decreased 84 sen to RM92.16, United Plantations Bhd shed 48 sen to RM34.52 and UMS Integration Ltd was down 32 sen to RM7.71.
Among the broader indices, the FBM Emas Index rose by 3.95 points to 12,703.17, and the FBMT 100 Index gained 8.71 points to 12,538.32.
The FBM Emas Shariah Index declined 44.86 points to 12,476.41, the FBM 70 Index slipped 94.10 points to 17,809.40, while the FBM ACE Index narrowed 14.63 points to 4,900.48.
Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index surged 179.79 points to 20,490.61, the Industrial Products and Services Index lowered 0.82 of a point to 187.11, and the Plantation Index weakened 48.48 points to 9,425.29, while the Energy Index edged down 2.06 points to 767.83.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man cleared of murder abetment fined RM2,500 for extorting restaurant owner
Article:
KLANG: A man acquitted of an abetment to murder charge three months ago was fined RM2,500 after admitting to extorting a female restaurant owner.
Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat fined Mok Kum Loon, 41, after he pleaded guilty to intentionally putting the 24-year-old victim in fear of injury to extort her.
The offence was committed less than a month after Mok was acquitted by the High Court of conspiring to
murder
the husband of a freelance female interpreter on April 24.
The magistrate also ordered him to serve six months in jail if he fails to pay the fine.
Mok committed the offence at 4pm at a restaurant in Lorong Senah Kawasan 18, here, on May 15.
He was charged under Section 385 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum seven jail term or a fine or whipping; or any two of those punishments upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Ahmad Sulfie Abu Hassan asked the court to impose a deterrent sentence.
In mitigation, defence lawyer Lavin Thiruchelvam sought a minimal fine, submitting that his client is unemployed and supporting his wife, children and sickly parents.
He said Mok was remorseful, had apologised for his actions and pledged not to reoffend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Malaysia needs teachers skilled in new technologies, rooted in human values
Article:
NILAI: Malaysia's education system must produce teachers who not only master emerging fields in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM), such as artificial intelligence (AI), but are also grounded in ethics and human values.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that in the face of rapid global change, education cannot simply continue with existing curricula and teaching methods but must remain dynamic to ensure the country does not fall behind in adopting new technologies.
However, he said that mastery of modern technology alone is insufficient unless it is balanced by the development of character, ethics, and human values among educators and students.
"As I put it, we must 'reach for the skies while keeping our roots on the ground', mastering new technologies without neglecting religion, culture, morality and human values.
"If we want to enhance the country's capabilities, our first priority must be education, followed by the ability to quickly adapt to changes taking place around the world," he said in his speech at the Temu Anwar programme with students and staff of the Teacher Education Institute, Technical Education Campus (IPGKPT) in Bandar Enstek today.
Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and IPGKPT director Dr Kartini Abdul Mutalib.
The prime minister also warned that education would fail to achieve its true purpose if teachers or students were influenced by sentiments of hatred towards other races and religions.
Anwar said Malaysia's multiracial society must strengthen understanding among different races and religions, as diversity should not become a cause for division.
"No country in the world, no matter how great, can remain secure by sowing seeds of racial or religious hatred. What must be upheld are human values.
"If we want to build this country, there must be tolerance, mutual respect and a commitment to upholding human values.
"Only then can we raise the quality of education and build a truly progressive society," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN-BN electoral understanding aims to wrest Negri Sembilan from PH
Article:
KOTA BARU: The 'understanding' between Perikatan Nasional and Barisan Nasional in the upcoming Negri Sembilan state election is aimed at wresting control from Pakatan Harapan.
Pas vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said the arrangement was intended to maximise the opposition's chances of defeating PH, which currently governs the state.
"In Negeri Sembilan, the objective is to take over the state government because PH is currently in power.
"Through this understanding, we hope to regain the state's political control," he said after delivering his sermon at Medan Ilmu here today.
Amar said the arrangement in Negri Sembilan, as well as in the recent Johor election, was an electoral understanding rather than a formal political alliance.
"It is more of an understanding than full cooperation. That is why there are seats where BN does not contest and seats where PN does not contest.
"This understanding only applies to Johor and Negri Sembilan for now. It is an ad hoc arrangement.
"Any similar approach in other states will depend on future circumstances," said the Panchor assemblyman.
Amar described the Johor arrangement as a positive development, saying it had contributed to BN's stronger-than-expected performance.
He said early projections had indicated BN would win only about 33 seats, but the understanding with PN helped the coalition secure 48 seats.
"It was a significant improvement. As Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has said, our larger objective is to prevent PH from remaining in power, and the Johor result showed that such an understanding can produce positive outcomes," he said.
Amar said the Negri Sembilan arrangement should be viewed as an initial step and a test case for possible future cooperation.
"We want to see how successful it will be and whether it is accepted by supporters at the grassroots level. If it works well and benefits the country, we may consider expanding it in the future.
"But it is still too early to say how far it can go because we do not yet know what will happen," he said.
The 16th Negri Sembilan election is scheduled for Aug 1, with nomination day set for July 18. All 36 state seats will be contested.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan election: PH banking on state government's track record
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Pakatan Harapan will focus on highlighting its performance record in the Negri Sembilan election rather than getting involved in the internal affairs of other political parties.
PKR Youth deputy chief Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari said PH's focus now was to ensure voters assessed the performance of the Negri Sembilan government rather than being drawn into the political manoeuvring of other parties.
He said the state government had proven its ability to administer effectively through various achievements, including development and economic growth, which formed the basis of PH's confidence in securing another mandate from the people.
"PH's approach is to demonstrate performance-based politics, and we do not want to interfere in whatever other parties choose to do.
"Our approach is not to find faults or attack our opponents, but to highlight what the Negri Sembilan government has implemented for the benefit of the people and ensure this continuity continues after the state election," he said after the Madani Kita programme at Padang Awam Bandar Puteri Jaya here today.
Two days ago, Barisan Nasional had announced that it would contest 25 state seats, while Perikatan Nasional would contest 11 seats not contested by BN, indirectly indicating cooperation between the two coalitions in the Negri Sembilan election.
On PH's chances of retaining Negri Sembilan, Dr Taufiq, who is also youth and sports minister, said the coalition was confident voters would make their choice based on the state government's track record rather than political controversies.
"Insya-Allah, we are very positive. The people of Negri Sembilan are capable of judging who is the best to lead the state government after the upcoming state election," he said.
Meanwhile, he said the Madani Kita programme served as a platform for the government to engage directly with the people by sharing various initiatives and assistance, as well as listening to issues and problems faced by the community.
He said the government had also allocated RM500,000 to Kampung Angkat Madani Kampung Bukit Kecil, RM200,000 for the Santuni Madani programme in Kampung Batu Dua, and RM100,000 to SJKT Taman Keladi through the Sekolah Angkat Madani programme.
"Today, several other contributions and aid were also handed over to the asnaf group and those in need, not only within the Sungai Petani parliamentary constituency but also to recipients selected from across Kedah.
"Most importantly, today's programme allows us to identify the problems and issues faced by local communities so that we can highlight these matters and find the best solutions.
"All policies implemented by the ministry and the government are not self-serving policies, but those that truly listen to the aspirations and voices of the people," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Most Bangkok bar fire victims died from smoke inhalation, autopsies find
Article:
BANGKOK: The majority of people killed in Bangkok's devastating bar fire died from smoke inhalation, forensic police told AFP on Friday after completing autopsies of all 33 victims.
The blaze swept through the popular Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao restaurant and bar in the Thai capital on Sunday night, quickly engulfing the venue.
Autopsies found that 27 victims died from carbon monoxide and cyanide poisoning after inhaling smoke, according to Wiroon Supasingsiripreecha, commander of the Institute of Forensic Medicine.
The gases are produced by fires and "when inhaled, block oxygen from reaching the blood, resulting in death", he told AFP.
"If inhaled in large quantities, they can cause death within four minutes."
The remaining six victims died later in hospital from severe burn injuries, Wiroon said.
Authorities said the death toll on Friday remained at 33, with 77 people injured.
The bar owner was admitted to intensive care immediately after the fire, but it was not clear if he was still in hospital.
Senior police officer Siam Boonsom said on Thursday that investigators had found evidence the venue was using high-voltage electricity without approval, which could have been a factor in the fire.
In the wake of the blaze, police and Bangkok's governing body said they would inspect 1,000 entertainment venues across the capital within one month. Officials have so far ordered three bars to be temporarily closed for failing to meet safety standards.
Thailand's approach to health and safety regulations – particularly in bars and nightclubs – has long raised concerns.
In 2009, a blaze tore through Bangkok's Santika club during New Year celebrations, killing 67 people and injuring more than 200.
A building safety expert told AFP earlier that Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao appeared to have lacked the safety systems required for the large crowds and live music events it hosted.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt starts drafting legal framework for road accident compensation, Bill expected in November
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government has begun drafting amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 to allow courts to award compensation to victims of serious road crashes, with motorists convicted of dangerous traffic offences set to bear greater responsibility for the harm they cause.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the cabinet had agreed in principle to the proposal at its meeting on Wednesday,  paving the way for stakeholder consultations before the Bill is tabled in Parliament.
He said the proposed amendment would introduce, for the first time, a legal mechanism allowing compensation to be awarded during criminal proceedings, while also strengthening the Road Safety Demerit Points System (Kejara).
Loke said the amendments were aimed at ensuring offenders were held accountable not only through imprisonment, fines or licence disqualification, but also for the impact of their actions on victims and their families.
"The cabinet has agreed in principle to amend the Road Transport Act once again to create a compensation mechanism for road crash victims and strengthen the Kejara demerit points system.
"This is to ensure offenders are not only punished through imprisonment, fines or suspension of their driving licences, but are also held responsible for the harm caused to victims and their families," he said at a press conference at the Transport Ministry here today.
Loke said the proposed mechanism would cover offences involving dangerous or reckless driving, including cases involving drivers under the influence of alcohol or drugs.
He said the amount of compensation would not be fixed by the government but determined by the courts based on the facts and merits of each case.
"The court will consider factors such as the seriousness of the offence, the injuries sustained or loss of life, the losses suffered by the victim or next of kin, as well as the offender's ability to pay."
Loke stressed that the proposed compensation mechanism would not affect victims' existing rights to pursue insurance claims or civil lawsuits.
Instead, he said, it would provide an additional avenue for victims and their families to obtain justice without having to initiate lengthy and costly civil proceedings.
"At present, many victims or their families are unable to pursue justice because they have to file separate civil suits, engage lawyers and bear legal costs.
"What we want is to incorporate an element of compensation into the criminal justice process itself."
He said the proposal had received broad support from cabinet members, including Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and ministers with legal backgrounds, although further refinements were needed before the amendments could be tabled in Parliament.
"The principle has been agreed. Now we need to work out the detailed legal framework and implementation mechanism."
Loke said the ministry would immediately begin engagement sessions with relevant ministries, enforcement agencies, the judiciary, the insurance industry and other stakeholders before drafting the amendments.
He said the government hoped to table the Bill during the final parliamentary meeting of the year, after the Budget debate, if preparations could be completed in time.
However, he stressed that the ministry would not rush the drafting process.
"This is a major reform that will have long-term implications for our legal framework and road safety system.
"We want to ensure the law is clear, fair and practical before it is brought back to Cabinet and subsequently tabled in Parliament."
Loke also proposed referring the Bill to a Parliamentary Special Select Committee after its first and second readings to allow lawmakers and stakeholders to scrutinise the legislation in greater detail.
He said the move reflected the government's commitment to producing a law that enjoyed bipartisan support and broad public confidence.
Alongside the compensation proposal, Loke said the government also intended to strengthen the Kejara system by improving the notification process for demerit points, introducing clearer mechanisms for appeals and administrative reviews, and allowing demerit points to be adjusted or removed if offences were not proven or convictions were overturned.
Asked whether the compensation provision would replace existing punishments, Loke said it would instead serve as an additional penalty.
"The existing penalties remain. If the offence carries imprisonment or a fine, those punishments will continue to apply. Compensation will be an additional responsibility imposed on offenders."
He added that the proposed amendments would apply only to offences committed after the law came into force, as criminal legislation could not operate retrospectively.
On concerns over offenders who might be unable to pay compensation, Loke said this was among the issues that would be addressed during the drafting process.
"These are precisely the details we need to refine. The policy has been agreed, but the implementation mechanism must be carefully worked out."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan election: 464 nomination forms sold ahead of nomination day
Article:
SEREMBAN: A total of 464 nomination forms for the Negri Sembilan election have been sold so far, said Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun.
Ramlan said only 70 prospective candidates had confirmed their participation by paying their deposits, and urged them to make payments early to ensure a smooth nomination process.
"The deadline for deposit payments is up until the nomination process tomorrow.
"As such, we hope all contestants will, if possible, make their payments early so that we will not be rushing against time tomorrow," he said.
He said this after inspecting preparations for the nomination process at centres for the Lenggeng, Nilai, Lobak, Temiang, Sikamat and Ampangan state constituencies at the Seremban City Council building this morning.
Ramlan said preparations for the nomination process involving the 36 state constituencies in Negri Sembilan were proceeding smoothly and had been completed ahead of nomination day tomorrow.
"This morning, we also conducted full rehearsal sessions at all nomination centres throughout the state to ensure the smooth running of the process tomorrow," he said.
Ramlan said 761 EC officers would be deployed tomorrow to oversee the nomination process at eight centres located in eight parliamentary constituencies.
He said that unlike the recent Johor election, which used nomination centres based on state constituencies, the Negri Sembilan polls would adopt a centralised approach based on the eight parliamentary constituencies, taking into account local needs and suitability for better coordination.
On security, he reminded all contesting political parties and their supporters to avoid any acts of provocation to ensure the process proceeds smoothly.
"I urge all prospective candidates to arrive early tomorrow to avoid congestion.
"Supporters are also urged to avoid any form of provocation to ensure harmony is maintained.
"Political parties are reminded to always comply with regulations and avoid common election offences throughout the campaign period," he said.
Based on the EC electoral roll updated as of June 4, 2026, a total of 889,490 voters are eligible to vote in the state election, comprising 867,151 ordinary voters, 16,884 military personnel and their spouses, and 5,455 police personnel as early voters.
Nominations will begin at 9am tomorrow and close an hour later, followed by the announcement of eligible candidates by returning officers.
The EC has set Aug 1 as polling day, while early voting will be held on July 28.
The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 5 after receiving the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
In the 15th Negri Sembilan election in 2023, Pakatan Harapan won 17 of the 36 state seats, followed by Barisan Nasional with 14 seats and Perikatan Nasional with five seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UMT turtle conservation efforts see fourfold nesting increase over 30 years
Article:
KUALA NERUS: Green turtle conservation efforts by Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT) at Pantai Chagar Hutang on Redang Island have yielded a three- to fourfold nesting increase over 30 years.
UMT Marine Conservation Centre head Associate Professor Dr Mohd Uzair Rusli said green turtle nests rose from 400 to 500 annually in the early 1990s to between 1,500 and 2,000 nests a year since 2016.
He said 4,609 turtles had been tagged over the period, with one female recorded returning to lay eggs 79 times over 28 years, making the site one of the world's most comprehensive long-term monitoring locations.
"Such long-term records are extremely rare worldwide. The population growth demonstrates that protecting nesting beaches, regulating egg collection, minimising human disturbance, and consistent monitoring have had a positive impact.
"However, although more turtles are returning to nest, researchers in the United Kingdom have found that these ancient reptiles are taking longer to return for their next breeding season. Findings published in the journal Royal Society Open Science show that the remigration interval is becoming longer," he said.
Uzair said UMT research suggests the change is linked to the condition of marine feeding habitats, as turtles migrate thousands of kilometres to foraging grounds before returning to nesting beaches years later.
He said depleted food sources or deteriorating habitats mean turtles require more time to accumulate sufficient energy to produce eggs.
His team is investigating two potential factors: climate change affecting the productivity of habitats like seagrass meadows, and increasing turtle populations at feeding grounds.
"As more turtles are successfully conserved, competition for food resources also increases. Both factors mean turtles need a longer recovery period before the next breeding season.
"Turtle conservation has entered a new phase. The real challenge lies at sea, as turtles spend more than 95 per cent of their life cycle in the water. Success depends on how well we protect their feeding habitats, seagrass meadows, migratory routes, and the marine ecosystem," he said.
Uzair added that the Chagar Hutang Turtle Sanctuary, established in 1993, has evolved into an ecosystem comprising the university, researchers, volunteers, local rangers, students, the local community, and strategic partners.
The sanctuary's daily operations are led by a chief researcher, supported by local rangers, postgraduate students, research assistants, industrial trainees, and more than 7,000 volunteers.
"This approach has enabled Pantai Chagar Hutang to continue operating despite limited resources. It is a community-based conservation model led by UMT.
"We are also strengthening the Environmental Sustainability Waqf Fund in collaboration with UMT Ihsan and RHB Islamic to establish a sustainable source of funding, ensuring research, conservation, student training, and community development can continue without relying entirely on short-term grants," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fact check: Trump rehashes false 2020 election fraud allegations
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump resurrected long-debunked claims about fraud and foreign interference in the 2020 election, alleging in an address Thursday that China stole millions of voter files and suggesting Venezuela could manipulate American voting machines.
The White House declassified intelligence documents as Trump doubled down on his assertion that the election he lost to Joe Biden was "stolen," which has never been substantiated.
More than 60 lawsuits failed to uncover fraud capable of changing the 2020 result, while elections officials and members of Trump's own administration repeatedly rejected his claims.
Rick Hasen, an election law expert at the University of California, Los Angeles, told AFP that Trump's speech consisted mostly of "recycled and debunked claims."
"What Trump did not do was even purport to show a single ineligible voter voted in the 2020 election, or that any voting machines were actually compromised," he wrote on his blog.
Here is a fact-check of some of Trump's claims:
- DATA COMPROMISED BY CHINA -
Trump accused China of "the largest compromise of election data in history, resulting in China's illicit acquisition of 220 million US voter files."
He later claimed China attempted "to manufacture illegal ballots for Joe Biden."
In the United States, however, voter files are largely matters of public record that states are required to maintain and which are regularly sold.
A declassified March 2021 report from the country's leading intelligence agencies reported "no indications that any foreign actor attempted to alter any technical aspect of the voting process."
The report said the agencies concluded with "high confidence" that Beijing "did not deploy interference efforts and considered but did not deploy influence efforts intended to change the outcome."
A separate government assessment similarly determined that there was "no evidence that any foreign government-affiliated actor" compromised the election.
Such a conspiracy to create thousands of fake voters would require fabricated Social Security entries, residences and identification, all while evading detection, former Maricopa County recorder Stephen Richer wrote on X.
- VENEZUELA -
Trump claimed Venezuela's elections were rigged under president Nicolas Maduro – and suggested US voting machines were vulnerable to similar manipulation.
The comments echoed conspiracy theories Trump allies pushed in 2020 alleging a Venezuelan plot to flip US votes through the elections software company Smartmatic.
Smartmatic – whose technology was only deployed in a single uncontested county during the 2020 election – later won settlements and defamation lawsuits over the false claims.
The documents the White House released Thursday say Venezuelan officials "developed sustained interest and likely some capability in manipulating electronic voting systems," but that the intelligence "did not definitively confirm that large-scale electronic fraud was successfully executed in specific Venezuelan elections."
They add that "neither Smartmatic nor the Venezuelan Government had the capability" to predictably "manipulate the outcome of an election outside of Venezuela."
"Nothing presented shows evidence of any vote manipulation," Charles Stewart, an elections expert at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, told AFP.
- NONCITIZEN VOTERS -
Trump claimed hundreds of thousands of non-citizens were registered to vote.
Non-citizen voting is illegal and has been shown by both election audits and independent research to be exceedingly rare. Several safeguards prevent non-citizens from voting.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pickup truck driver claims trial to dangerous driving over fatal Bentong crash
Article:
RAUB: A pickup truck driver claimed trial at the magistrate's court here today to charges of causing the death of a motorcyclist and seriously injuring the victim's six-year-old son through dangerous driving in Mempaga, Bentong, on July 14.
Hairizal Ahmad, 44, pleaded not guilty after the two charges were read separately before Magistrate Siti Aisyah Ahmad.
He was charged with driving dangerously, causing an accident that resulted in the death of Zaiful Arimey Zainal Abidin, 33.
For the second charge, Hairizal was accused of driving recklessly in a manner that endangered Zaiful Arimey's six-year-old son, Putera Nik Zarif Aqil, who was riding pillion.
According to the charge, Hairizal allegedly entered the opposite lane before colliding with the
motorcycle
carrying the father and son, who were at a junction.
The offence was allegedly committed at Km15, Jalan Utama Mempaga, near SMA Persekutuan Bentong, at 10.25pm on July 14.
The first charge, under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, carries a jail term of between five and 10 years, a fine of between RM20,000 and RM50,000, and disqualification from holding or obtaining a driving licence for at least five years upon conviction.
The second charge, under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, carries a maximum jail term of five years and a fine of between RM5,000 and RM15,000 upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zamharir Muhammad Zuhid appeared for the prosecution, while lawyer R. Vikraman represented the accused.
Magistrate Siti Aisyah allowed bail at RM10,000 for both charges and fixed Aug 17 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 19-year-old Rohingya woman charged with murder over newborn's death
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: A 19-year-old Rohingya woman was charged with murder at the Johor Baru magistrate's court today for allegedly throwing her newborn baby out of a hotel window.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the woman allegedly threw the newborn from a second-floor hotel room window into an alley in Bukit Indah.
He said police received information about the discovery of a plastic-wrapped newborn in an alley at Taman Nusa Idaman, Bukit Indah, at 2.39am on July 5.
"Following the discovery, police arrested a 34-year-old local man at the scene at 4am on the same day before detaining the woman at a nearby hotel.
"Investigations revealed that the newborn was the child of the female suspect, who had given birth in the hotel room toilet following a relationship with her former boyfriend.
"The suspect is believed to have placed the newborn in a black plastic bag before throwing it into the alley," he said in a statement today.
Kumarasan said the male suspect, who has two previous criminal and drug-related records, would serve as a prosecution witness, while the female suspect had no prior record.
Both tested negative for drugs.
The man was remanded for four days from July 5 to 8, while the woman was remanded for 14 days under Section 51(5)(b) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 to assist investigations.
The case was referred to the Johor deputy public prosecutor's office, resulting in the woman being charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder.
Upon conviction, the offence carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, and not fewer than 12 strokes of the rotan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing Kota Tinggi teen found dead after nearly week-long search
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: A Form Three student who was reported missing since Sunday was found dead after his motorcycle was believed to have skidded at Km8.2 Jalan Kluang-Kota Tinggi early this morning.
The victim, identified as Muhammad Nasrulhaq Kamaruzaman, 15, was found after nearly a week of search efforts by his family and villagers.
His aunt, Nooranaliza Abd Jalil, 43, said she was informed that Muhammad left home at 11pm last Saturday to visit a friend in Kampung Temenin Baru.
"Before the incident, he was said to have made a video call with a friend, but the call suddenly disconnected. The video call at 4.26am on Sunday was the last time he could be contacted," she said.
Nooranaliza said she returned to her hometown at Felda Simpang Waha on the day of the Johor election last Saturday, and that was her last meeting with the teenager.
"He did not show any changes in behaviour. He was a quiet person," she said.
She said the family and villagers had spent nearly a week searching for Nasrulhaq since he was reported missing, but had accepted his death.
Meanwhile, district police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said police received information from members of the public about the discovery of the victim at around 2.30am today.
He said initial investigations found that the Yamaha 135LC motorcycle ridden by Nasrulhaq was believed to have skidded on its own at Km8.2 Jalan Kluang-Kota Tinggi.
The accident caused serious injuries to his body and legs, and he was pronounced dead at the scene.
"The victim's body was sent to the Forensic Department at Kota Tinggi Hospital for investigation procedures under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987," he said in a statement.
Yusof advised road users to remain vigilant and comply with traffic laws and regulations to ensure safety.
A Felda Simpang Waha resident who helped locate the teenager's body said villagers had continued searching for the teenager since he was reported missing.
"He was the same age as my child, so I could not bear to leave him after hearing he was missing," said the resident, who declined to be named.
He said he was patrolling the area on a motorcycle early this morning when he noticed a smell near the roadside.
He then sought help from other road users to search a nearby oil palm plantation area near an industrial zone, where they eventually found Nasrulhaq.
"With Allah's permission, Muhammad Nasrulhaq was found early this morning. We then contacted the police and his family for further action," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan election: Berjasa to contest Nilai seat after PN snub
Article:
SEREMBAN: Parti Barisan Jemaah Islamiah Se-Malaysia (Berjasa) will contest the Nilai seat in the upcoming Negri Sembilan election.
Its president Zamani Ibrahim said the decision was made after closely monitoring candidate announcements by other contesting parties and taking the findings of the party's own research into account.
He said previous studies indicated that one of the factors contributing to low voter turnout among Malays was the absence of a Malay candidate in Malay-majority areas.
"Berjasa is here to listen to and understand the aspirations of Malay-Muslim voters in Negri Sembilan amidst the current challenging national political landscape.
"Based on our principles and to enliven the country's democratic system, Berjasa decided to contest in Nilai to offer a capable Muslim candidate to the voters," he said in a statement here today.
Zamani said Nilai was chosen as it is a suitable starting point to field a Malay candidate in a Malay-majority area.
He said the current scenario, which sees all other candidates announced for the Nilai seat being non-Malay, also factored into the decision.
"Berjasa remains committed to the Malay unity agenda and will not field candidates in Malay-majority areas that already have Malay candidates from Barisan Nasional, Pas, and Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan)," he said.
The Nilai seat is currently held by Arul Kumar Jambunathan from PH-DAP, who won with a majority of 10,889 votes in the 2023 Negri Sembilan election.
In this state election, Arul Kumar will defend the seat, while BN is fielding MCA candidate Lai Chien Kong.
Previously, Zamani said his party intended to contest eight seats and was ready to cooperate with Pas under PN. However, following last night's candidate announcement, no candidate from Berjasa was named.
The
11 state seats
contested by PN only involve Pas contesting in five seats, Wawasan (four seats), and one seat each for Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fake cake link costs woman RM1.39 million
Article:
MIRI: A woman lost RM1.39 million after falling victim to a scam syndicate that used a fake cake-ordering link to gain access to her company's bank accounts.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said police received a report yesterday from the 43-year-old victim.
He said the woman had come across a cake advertisement on social media and clicked on a link provided to view the menu and place an order.
"After clicking the link, the victim's mobile phone screen suddenly went black and stopped functioning.
"At 10am on Wednesday, the victim checked with her bank and discovered unauthorised money transfers from two bank accounts belonging to her company," he said in a statement today.
Farhan said initial investigations found that 19 transactions were made to 15 different bank accounts on July 14, involving a total of RM1,389,132.
He said the case was being investigated under Section 4(1) of the Computer Crimes Act 1997, which carries a maximum fine of RM150,000 or imprisonment of up to 10 years, or both, upon conviction.
He advised the public to remain vigilant against increasingly prevalent phishing tactics, particularly those involving suspicious links on social media.
He also reminded the public not to click on dubious links and only download applications from official platforms, and to avoid sharing passwords, PINs or one-time passwords (OTPs) with anyone to prevent falling victim to cybercrime.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PBAPP: New water tariffs essential to avert future shortages
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang's revised water tariffs are necessary to fund urgent water supply projects and prevent future shortages as demand continues to grow, Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) said.
PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said additional revenue from the revised tariffs, which took effect on July 1, would be channelled towards critical projects under the Water Contingency Plan 2030 (WCP 2030).
He said about 82 per cent of Penang households consuming 35m³ or less of water a month would pay a maximum of RM2.55 more monthly, or about eight sen a day.
Business consumers using 500m³ of water a month would pay an additional RM77.70, or RM2.59 a day.
"The additional cost is a reasonable price to ensure Penang has sufficient water supply infrastructure to support continued socio-economic growth," he said today.
His statement came in the wake of Bagan member of parliament Lim Guan Eng's letter to Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow urging the state government to postpone the tariff increase by another year.
Lim argued that the projected RM20 million in additional annual revenue would make only a limited contribution towards the
billions
of ringgit required for water infrastructure, and suggested the state seek federal grants or loans instead.
Pathmanathan said the projects to be funded include the construction of the new 114 million litres per day (MLD) Mengkuang Park Water Treatment Plant (WTP), land acquisition for the 136MLD Sungai Prai WTP, upgrades to the 1,342MLD Sungai Dua WTP, the proposed Sungai Muda WTP, and the Macallum-Bukit Dumbar pipeline.
"Delaying the tariff revision further will postpone funding for these projects and increase the risk of water shortages before 2030," he said.
Responding to calls to defer the tariff hike, Pathmanathan said claims that PBAPP could wait another year or rely solely on federal funding were misleading.
He said the federal government was already undertaking five raw water resource projects in Penang worth an estimated RM838 million, while expanding treated water capacity remained the responsibility of state water operators.
He added that PBAPP's RM5 billion sukuk programme, launched last year, was necessary because annual profits alone were insufficient to finance the estimated RM2.1 billion required for the remaining WCP 2030 projects.
Pathmanathan said Penang's gross domestic product (GDP) grew by 7.3 per cent from RM121.4 billion in 2024 to RM130.3 billion in 2025, while the state's GDP per capita reached RM80,584, or 36.2 per cent above the national average.
Despite this, he said Penang continued to have among the country's lowest water tariffs, with domestic consumers paying an average of RM0.93 per m³ for the first 35m³ and non-domestic consumers RM2.28 per m³ for the first 500m³.
Pathmanathan said the revised tariffs are expected to generate an additional RM20 million in annual profit, which would be reinvested in water infrastructure.
He said PBA Holdings Bhd (PBAHB) had allocated about 87 per cent of its RM114.5 million profit after tax for 2025 as capital expenditure for water supply projects this year, with the balance mainly distributed as dividends.
He said Penang's water demand is projected to increase from 865MLD in 2025 to at least 1,162MLD by 2032, driven by industrial expansion, new property developments and major projects such as Batu Kawan Industrial Park 3 and Silicon Island.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Langkawi daycare centre under probe for allegedly starving, locking children in toilet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating child abuse allegations at a childcare centre in Kuah, Langkawi, following reports that children were deprived of food and locked in a toilet.
The allegations gained traction after a Threads post today by user @_misaharoff, who claimed her sister's child was traumatised after being locked in a toilet as punishment.
"They allegedly locked my nephew in the toilet and attempted to torture him. He is now traumatised and screams every day whenever he remembers what happened," she wrote.
The user claimed several parents had since lodged police reports and were awaiting the outcome of investigations.
"I don't usually make posts like this, but this has gone too far. Imagine children allegedly being denied food, drinks and milk, then forced to sleep before being locked in the toilet," she wrote.
Bernama reported that Langkawi police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Azhar Nuruddin confirmed that police received a report from a former teacher at the centre alleging improper treatment of children.
He said the allegations included claims that some children were made to sleep on the floor without mattresses, while others were locked inside a toilet.
However, Khairul Azhar said police had not received any reports from parents alleging their children had suffered injuries or other harm linked to the claims.
He said investigators were reviewing CCTV footage and recording statements from witnesses to verify the allegations.
The case is being investigated under Section 31(1) of the Child Act 2001.
The social media post has garnered more than 239,000 views, drawing widespread criticism from netizens who urged parents to personally inspect childcare centres, ensure they are registered, and verify that surveillance cameras are functioning.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan election: Fair weather expected on nomination day, says MetMalaysia
Article:
SEREMBAN: Most parts of Negri Sembilan are expected to enjoy fine weather tomorrow morning for the state's 16th election nomination day.
Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said fair weather is expected in the morning in Jelebu, Jempol, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin, while Port Dickson and Seremban are expected to experience rain.
He said, however, that thunderstorms are forecast in most districts later in the afternoon.
"Based on current data, the rain is expected to be temporary and not significant. It is expected to be brief passing showers rather than prolonged heavy rainfall.
"Minimum temperatures across the state are expected to range between 23 and 24 degrees Celsius, while maximum temperatures are forecast at 32 to 33 degrees Celsius," he said.
Hisham advised the public, including candidates, supporters and election workers, to obtain the latest weather updates through the MetMalaysia website at www.met.gov.my.
The Negri Sembilan election will begin tomorrow with the nomination process at eight nomination centres from 9am to 10am, followed by the official announcement of the confirmed candidates by the returning officers.
So far, Pakatan Harapan has announced that it will contest all 36 state seats, with PKR allocated 16 seats, followed by DAP with 11 and Amanah with nine.
Meanwhile, Barisan Nasional will contest 25 seats, comprising 16 from Umno, seven from MCA and two from MIC.
Perikatan Nasional has also announced that it will contest 11 seats, fielding five candidates from Pas, four from Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan), and one each from Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party.
Also announcing their participation in the election are Parti Orang Asli Malaysia, Parti Sosialis Malaysia and Parti Barisan Jemaah Islamiah Se-Malaysia, each fielding one candidate.
Later today, Bersatu is expected to announce its list of candidates for the state election.
The 36-seat Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 5, with the Election Commission setting July 28 as early voting day and Aug 1 as polling day.
According to the electoral roll issued by the commission, 889,490 voters are eligible to cast their ballots in the upcoming state election.
Of the total, 867,151 are ordinary voters, while 16,884 are military personnel and their spouses, and 5,455 are police personnel eligible for early voting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan keeps male-only throne despite public support for woman emperor
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's parliament enacted changes to the imperial succession law on Friday but maintained the bar on a woman becoming emperor despite surveys suggesting wide public support for the idea.
The future of the imperial household – mythically descended from the Shinto sun goddess Amaterasu – hinges currently on Prince Hisahito, the 19-year-old nephew of serving Emperor Naruhito, 66.
If Hisahito – barely out of school and currently studying biology and insects and who is not married – has no son, then under the rules as they stand he will have no heir and the bloodline will end.
The bill, passed by a large majority by the upper house on Friday, allows the adoption of male distant relatives over 15 back into the imperial family – as long as they are single.
It also lets women keep their royal status after marrying a commoner, something which is already allowed for men.
Japan's Imperial Household Law, in place since 1947, does not allow women to ascend the Chrysanthemum Throne, and the right to the throne can only be passed through the male line.
This rules out popular Princess Aiko, 24, daughter of Naruhito, or Hisahito's two elder sisters, ever becoming emperor.
The legislation passed after considerable wrangling within the conservative ruling party of Sanae Takaichi, Japan's first woman prime minister, who opposes female succession.
Seiichiro Murakami, a veteran of the ruling Liberal Democratic Party, said after the bill passed the lower house on July 10 that it was "utterly outrageous" to rule out the possibility of Aiko as emperor.
Former imperial family member Asahiro Kuni, 81, has said it would be unrealistic to adopt distant male relatives, adding he would advise his grandchildren to refuse such a proposal.
Kuni is a member of one of the 11 imperial branch families that left the imperial register after World War II.
"By the age of 15, a person has grown up breathing the air of freedom," Kuni told the Asahi Shimbun daily.
"I think it would be difficult to adapt to life in the imperial family."
"There might be people who want to join the imperial family, but if they understood the hardships of life as a royal member, they probably wouldn't say such a thing," Kuni added.
The top-selling Yomiuri Shimbun daily, usually a vocal supporter of the LDP, also criticised the government in a recent editorial.
The imperial family now has 16 members in total, including five men – retired emperor Akihito, who is 92, his 90-year-old brother, the 66-year-old emperor, his brother, and Hisahito.
An Asahi Shimbun poll in May showed 72 percent of respondents in favour of changing the rules to allow women to ascend the throne.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: At least 14 dead in Peru road accident
Article:
LIMA: At least 14 people died and five others were injured on Thursday when a van plunged off a roadway in northern Peru, authorities told AFP.
"We have reports of 14 people dead and five injured after a minibus fell into a ravine," said Ricardo Chilon, head of institutional communications for the San Juan municipality, located more than 800km north of Lima in the Cajamarca region.
Police and fire crews arrived at the scene of the accident, outside San Juan, to recover the victims' bodies, including those of the driver and a child, Chilon said.
The vehicle was carrying 19 people from the San Juan district to Ciudad de Dios. The cause of the accident is under investigation.
Fire Department official Edson Roman told reporters that six bodies had yet to be recovered.
"The rescue operation is very difficult for us. The vehicle is wedged in a ravine," Roman said from the crash site.
Road accidents are common in Peru because of poor road conditions, a lack of police enforcement and human error, primarily involving speeding.
More than 3,000 people were killed on Peru's roads last year, according to official figures.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sectors across the board spurs Malaysia's 5.8pct economic growth in Q2 2026: DOSM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy expanded by 5.8 per cent in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2 2026), following the 5.4 per cent growth in the preceding quarter, according to the Statistics Department.
In its Advance Gross Domestic Product (GDP) Estimates for Q2 2026 report, it said the performance was supported by growth in almost all economic sectors, except for the agriculture sector, which experienced a contraction.
"Overall, Malaysia's economy grew at 5.6 per cent for the first half of the year (2H 2026), compared to 4.5 per cent in 2H 2025," it said in a statement today.
It said the services sector remained the main driver of economic growth during the quarter, with an increase of 5.4 per cent (Q1 2026: 5.6 per cent), boosted by the wholesale and retail trade; information and communication; and transportation and storage sub-sectors.
The department reported that the manufacturing sector strengthened further to 7.5 per cent (Q1 2026: 5.9 per cent), driven by export-oriented industries, particularly the production of electrical, electronic and optical products, as well as petroleum, chemicals, rubber and plastics products.
"The performance of the mining and quarrying sector has also rebounded to record an increase of 10.2 per cent (Q1 2026: -2.1 per cent), driven by the natural gas sub-sector.
"The construction sector grew moderately at 6.6 per cent (Q1 2026: 7.7 per cent), supported by the non-residential buildings sub-sector, particularly data centre construction projects and specialised construction activities," it said.
Meanwhile, the department said the agriculture sector contracted by 3.7 per cent in Q2 2026 compared to a growth of 2.6 per cent in the previous quarter, following a decline in all sub-sectors, except for other agriculture and livestock.
It said the preliminary GDP, which provides a detailed and comprehensive analysis of economic performance for Q2 2026 will be released on Aug 14, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL anti-begging sweep nets 25 across Kuala Lumpur tourist belt
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said it took enforcement action against 25 people during a joint anti-begging operation across several city centre hotspots, with five later handed over to other government agencies for further action.
In a statement posted on its official Facebook page today, DBKL said the operation was carried out last night around Jalan Bukit Bintang, Jalan Sultan Ismail, Jalan Alor and Leboh Pudu.
The operation, which began at 8pm, involved the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department (JIM), the National Registration Department (JPN), the Social Welfare Department (JKM) and the National Security Council (MKN).
DBKL said those subjected to enforcement action comprised 13 Malaysians and 12 foreign nationals.
“Of the total, five individuals were handed over to the relevant agencies for further action, involving two individuals to the Immigration Department and three individuals to the Social Welfare Department,” it said.
DBKL said it would continue strengthening strategic cooperation with enforcement and government agencies to curb begging activities in the capital.
“DBKL will continue to strengthen strategic cooperation with the relevant agencies in efforts to curb begging activities while ensuring the peace, safety and well-being of city residents are always preserved,” it said.

Title: Six charged with possessing more than six tonnes of subsidised cooking oil without licence
Article:
SHAH ALAM, July 17 — Six men, including a Malaysian, were charged in the Sessions Court here today with possessing a total of 6,443 kilogrammes of subsidised cooking oil without a valid licence last Tuesday.
Lorry driver Moo Teck Lim, 50, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read to him in Mandarin before Judge Fatimah Zahari.
The charges against four others, all Myanmar nationals, aged between 21 and 36, will be read out to them again on August 17 after the court was informed that they do not understand the Malay language and require the services of an interpreter.
According to the charge, Moo and the four foreign nationals were allegedly found in possession of 4,845 kilogrammes of cooking oil under suspicious circumstances at a premises on Jalan Apollo, Bandar Pinggiran Subang, at 3.30pm on July 14.
They were charged under Regulation 3(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974, an offence punishable under Section 22(1) of the Control of Supplies Act 1961, read together with Section 21 of the same Act and Section 34 of the Penal Code.
Upon conviction, they are liable to a fine of up to RM1 million, imprisonment for up to three years, or both. For subsequent offences, the penalty is a fine of up to RM5 million, imprisonment for up to five years, or both.
The prosecution was conducted by prosecuting officer Sallim Wahid, of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN), while the accused were unrepresented.
The court granted Moo bail of RM15,000 with one surety and imposed additional conditions requiring him to report to the KPDN office in Putrajaya once a month and surrender his passport to the court.
The case was fixed for mention on August 17 for the submission of documents and the attendance of a Myanmar interpreter.
In separate proceedings, Indian national Senthil Vadivel, 49, pleaded not guilty to a charge of possessing 1,598 kilogrammes of cooking oil without a licence at a premises on Jalan Bakti 25/15 here at 5.40pm on the same day.
He was charged under the same provisions of the Control of Supplies Act 1961.
KPDN prosecuting officer Nur Abyan Anuar appeared for the prosecution, while Senthil was represented by lawyer Mark Jeyakumar Michael.
Judge Fatimah allowed bail of RM40,000 with two local sureties, subject to the additional conditions that the accused report to the KPDN office in Putrajaya during the first week of every month and surrender his passport to the court.
The court fixed August 24 for case mention, as well as the submission of documents and the chemistry report. — Bernama

Title: Police warn against spreading false or provocative social media posts during Negeri Sembilan polls
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 17 — Police have reminded the public to use social media responsibly throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, including by refraining from spreading defamatory, seditious, insulting, provocative or false content.
Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) viewed seriously any dissemination of content that could undermine public order, threaten national security or disrupt social harmony.
He said the public was advised not to misuse social media platforms by uploading, sharing or circulating statements that could incite hatred, hostility or tensions involving race, religion, the royal institution or any parties involved in the electoral process.
“PDRM will not compromise with any individual found to have committed an offence, and stern action will be taken in accordance with the relevant laws.
“This includes the Penal Code, the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 (Act 588), the Sedition Act 1948, as well as other applicable laws,” he said in a statement here today.
Alzafny said police would intensify monitoring of social media content throughout the election period, and any breaches of the law would be investigated regardless of the background or status of those involved.
He urged the public to obtain information from credible sources, not to be easily influenced by provocation, and to work together to preserve peace, public order and national harmony, thereby ensuring that the Negeri Sembilan state election is conducted in a peaceful, orderly and lawful manner. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar: Biggest barrier to reform is resistance to change
Article:
NILAI, July 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the biggest challenge in implementing national reforms stems not from a lack of technology or expertise, but from the reluctance of certain quarters to embrace change, including among members of the elite who continue to defend a culture of corruption and systemic weaknesses.
He said his more than three years of leading the government had shown that efforts to reform the system and eradicate corruption often faced resistance from those who had grown comfortable with longstanding practices.
Anwar said the government’s efforts to strengthen governance and combat corruption might not be popular, but they must continue to ensure the country’s administrative system becomes more transparent, accountable and effective.
“Many people are uncomfortable when we seek to reform the system. However, no system is ever perfect and it must always be improved. That is the demand of religion, culture and civilisation.
“When we seek to reform the system and eradicate a culture of corruption that has come to be regarded as normal, that is when resistance to change emerges. They may be modern in their attire and lifestyle, but the reluctance to change remains because change will challenge their position and the status quo,” he said.
He said this while speaking at the
Temu Anwar
programme with students and staff of the Technical Education Campus of the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG) in Bandar Enstek here today.
Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Technical Education Campus, Institute of Teacher Education director Kartini Abdul Mutalib.
Anwar said that, according to the theory of change, society is generally divided into two groups: those with a propensity to change and those who exhibit resistance to change.
The prime minister said the perception that only rural communities or the less educated are reluctant to embrace change is not necessarily accurate, as highly educated individuals, intellectuals and those in positions of power can display the same attitude.
